observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, and to recognize its related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.

We also recommended that the Supply Officer prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, within five days after the end of each month, supported with pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips issued to end users as basis in recording the reduction of the Inventory account and its related expense account.

We further recommended that the MDRRMO, MSWDO and other officials, who accepted the relief goods and took charge with its distribution, submit a sworn certification that all the relief goods were fully distributed to the intended recipients and that no remaining stocks were in their custody as of December 31, 2020.  Any remaining stocks should be reported to the Accountant for recording in the Inventory account.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit within three (3) days, the following:

A written explanation on why the requirements mandated in GPPB Circular 01-2020 were not complied prior to the award and payment of the transactions listed in Table 1; and

BAC Resolution recommending the award of the contracts, Notices of Award, Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit, and the Tax Returns and the Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) of contracts above P500,000.00 of the transactions listed in Table 1, otherwise, the transactions shall be issued with Notice of Suspension.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer process transactions only when duly supported with the prescribed necessary documents to avoid audit suspension or disallowance.
observations.reason: Please refer to related Observation Nos. 3,9 & 11 on Procurement in Part II of this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all collectors and the Municipal Treasurer be strictly required to prepare Monthly RAAF and Consolidated RAAF including their submission for audit within the prescribed period.

We also recommended that an Executive Order be issued creating the Monitoring & Evaluation Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a certified list of the taxpayers, as well as its total amount due and collectible for the year 2017, and thereafter, provide such duly certified list at the beginning of every year.  Accordingly, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record immediately the receivables as soon as the Municipal Treasurer provide such list.  Further, we require that the Municipal Treasurer update its real property tax account register, and render collection notices to the concerned taxpayers.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer maintain Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations as mandated by COA Circular No. 2005-001 dated February 28, 2005 and record obligations in the registries before approving the obligations request/slip for proper monitoring of the status of appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

Management submit the written request for write-off of dormant receivables and unliquidated cash advances duly supported with the requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

The Municipal Accountant adjust the books of accounts to restore the beginning balances derecognized amounting to P4,853,667.65 and P2,435,277.68 for the accounts Advances to Officers of and Employees and Advances to Special Disbursing Officer, respectively.

The Municipal Accountant comply with all the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that management return immediately to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized DAP fund of P3 million in line with the Supreme Court en banc Decision promulgated on July 1, 2014, as modified in its Resolution promulgated on February 3, 2015.

We recommended that Management, through the HRMO, require the submission of the printout from the biometrics as supporting document of the manual Daily Time Record to be submitted regularly by all permanent officials and employees, co-terminus, casual, contractual, and Job Order workers of the agency.

We recommended that henceforth, the Inventory Committee undertake an annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of each ensuing year.

We recommended that:

Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, General Services Officer, and LDRRMC insure yearly with the GSIS the LGU’s insurable properties based on the annual inventory of insurable properties, using the unexpended/unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE funds.
 
Management appropriate in its budget for the payment of premiums for adequate insurance coverage with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS of the LGU’s insurable assets.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan:

Update immediately the Municipal Tax Ordinance to conform with the demands of time in strict compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 and its IRR.

Henceforth, update the Municipal Tax Ordinance every after five (5) years pursuant to Article 281 of the IRR of RA 7160.
We recommend that the deputized Collectors/Tellers remit their collection daily to the regular Revenue Collection Clerk who is accountable therefor.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor the Reconciliation Statements monthly.

We recommended that management:

Inform the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

Submit to the Auditor the contracts and their supporting documents within five (5) days from perfection.

We recommended that management:

Send a demand letter to Mr. Angelino Escuadra, the former Municipal Accountant, requiring him to make proper turn-over of records including the General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers of prior years and Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger Balances as of December 31, 2013. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the CY 2014 Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger Balances, and the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the CY 2014 SAAOB under the General Fund and Special Education Fund.

Require the Property Officer to (1) take charge of storing deliveries received and report on utilization and/or issuance of drugs, medicines, medical supplies and equipment (2) maintain Stock Cards on all types of drugs, medicines and medical supplies, and Property Cards for equipment for DRRM, and (3) prepare the Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment on issuance of PPE, and Inventory Custodian Slip for drugs, medicines, and medical supplies.

Otherwise, the corresponding transactions shall be disallowed after ninety (90) days from receipt of the Notice. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
create a new Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members that will be committed to conduct a complete annual physical count of LGU properties;
issue a directive to the newly created Inventory Committee to:
prepare, for his approval, a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the assignments of Committee members, schedules/location of inventory-taking and cut-off dates for the complete physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality over a three-month period in CY 2022;
start immediately, and complete during the year, the physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality, including road networks, in accordance with the procedures and schedule in the PIP; and
prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and submit the same to the Accountant for reconciliation with the book balances and to the Audit Team for verification; and
issue a directive to the Municipal Engineer to assist the Property Officer in the preparation of the Municipal Road Map, with proper labels, and to assist the Inventory Team in the conduct of physical count of all municipal roads as basis in their preparation of the RPCLRN.
We also recommended that the designated Property Officer prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all roads and its components and reconcile said Property Cards with the Accountant’s PPELC annually.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
obtain from the Leyte Provincial Health Office the actual information on the unit cost of the vaccines that the LGU will receive in the future in relation to its vaccination program; and
provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents from the Leyte Provincial Health Office relative to the receipt and distribution of vaccines, at the end of each month.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the vaccines received by the LGU, based on the documents submitted by the MHO, as part of its inventory using the perpetual inventory system.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC), through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, stop the inclusion of generic and vague projects with lumpsum appropriations in the Annual Investment Plan, otherwise, secure first the authorization from the Sanggunian Bayan prior to any disbursement from the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant discontinue the utilization of the 20% Development Fund for administrative expenses to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
transfer to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund the unexpended appropriation of CYs 2020 and 2021 totaling ₱3,704,451.89, after the enactment of an Ordinance approving its utilization; and
transfer, in the succeeding years, to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund account, the correct unexpended amount of the LDRRMF before the last working day of each year to be used for disaster management activities of the LGU within five years from the date of transfer.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer request for the enactment of an Ordinance to make available the unexpended appropriation of CYs 2020 and 2021 totaling ₱3,704,451.89, which were reverted to the surplus of the General Fund, prior to payments in CY 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor approves, and the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant process payments, for the utilization of the LDRRMF only for PPAs that are included in the LDRRMFIP and integrated in the AIP.
We further recommended that in future transactions, the MDRRMC, through the MDRRMO, incorporate in the annual/revised LDRRMFIP and the Annual/Supplemental Investment Program, all PPAs to be charged to LDRRMF, including subsequent changes thereon, duly supported with proper authority through Resolutions from MDRRMC, MDC and the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer:
maintain Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for QRF, MOOE and CO;
refrain from disbursing funds without available appropriations; and
limit its disbursements up to the amount that was explicitly authorized under the appropriation ordinance of the Municipality, otherwise the excess would be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal Accountant submit the following supporting documents to validate the eligibility and entitlement of the claimants, and to support the accuracy of the payments:
Pre-determined list by the Local Health Board of the frontline HRHs and HWs, including the BHWs, duly validated by an authorized DOH representative;
Duly accomplished DTRs of all recipients of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA; and
Certification of the Municipal Health Officer that the claimants were assigned at the Rural Health Unit or in the LGU’s quarantine facility.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Bids and Awards Committee:
include in the Annual Procurement Plan all ancillary supplies and services relative to the LGU’s COVID-19 Vaccination Program and revise or update the APP to cover emergency procurement or changes in modality; and
adopt the appropriate mode of procurement, especially during emergency cases.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant process Disbursements Vouchers for the purchase of ancillary supplies and services only when the items are included in the APP and the supporting documents are complete and bear all the necessary information.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately make the necessary adjustments in the books in CY 2021 and submit to the Audit Team a certified copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking up said adjustment.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, he already made the adjusting entries. However, to-date, he has yet to submit a certified copy of the JEV of such adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the Municipal Planning Development Council update its Annual Investment Plan and indicate therein specific development projects which are viable and necessary, with complete description as to the kind of project, its location and estimated appropriation in order to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issues a directive to the MPDO, the BAC and the Municipal Engineer, to implement and complete all PPAs during the year that it was identified in the AIP.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.  Please refer to Observation No. 4 of Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant seek the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan, by way of supplemental legislative authorization, so as to legitimize the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We also recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, thru the MDRRM Officer, include always in the LDRRMFIP, under a separate caption, the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF to ensure that disbursements therefrom are covered with appropriate authority.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer take the initiative to open and maintain a separate bank account as Special Trust Fund for the unexpended LDRRMF in prior years and transfer the equivalent amount from the General Fund to the new LDRRMF bank account.

Lastly, we recommended that the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant stop using the LDRRM Fund for administrative expenses and provide the MDRRM Office with the funds for such expenses in its annual budget to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.  Please refer to Observation No. 7 of Part II

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC Chairman, through its Secretariat, post in the PhilGEPS and GPPB website the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents on the procurement activities of the Municipality of Mayorga.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the BAC’s compliance with the on-line posting requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator and the Local Development Council (LDC) be required to regularly monitor the status of the project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time to ensure maximum utilization of LDRRMF especially Mitigation Fund.

We recommended that the Municipality be required strictly remit to BIR the correct and full amount of taxes withheld on or before its prescribed due date.  Accordingly, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant make an appropriate adjustment in the books of accounts regarding the amount of liquidated damages paid.

We recommended that Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job order personnel through Automated Teller Machine instead of cash.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant and to the Auditor a revised Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations for CY 2015, and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the SCBAA for CY 2015.


Require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile monthly their records to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the SCBAA to the Auditor in the ensuing years.

We recommend that management require the present Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the previous Municipal Accountants to verify the various discrepancies in amounts of real accounts in the CY 2014 financial statements and General Ledgers and to make proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and the next preceding Municipal Accountant in order to fully account and establish transactions and balances per books of each bank depository account of the municipality.

Prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements for CY 2014 and onwards in strict adherence to COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to avoid the accumulation of unadjusted reconciling items.

Maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank depository account maintained by the municipality.

Draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the Subsidiary and General Ledgers.

We recommended that management:

 Strictly observe the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 on the immediate settlement and liquidation of all outstanding cash advances granted before December 31, 2011.

Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the unreconciled difference of P304,708.14 between the General Ledger and Schedule of Cash Advances of the Advances to Officers and Employees account under the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to observe strictly the provisions in Section 04 (e), Volume I and Sections 09 and 10, Volume II of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002. Otherwise, we shall recommend for the imposition of the provisions of Section 122 of PD 1445 on the suspension of payment of salaries of concerned officials.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant record purchases of supplies and materials as Asset first and recorded the corresponding Expense account only when these inventories are sold, consumed, exchanged or distributed as required in PPSAS 12.  He shall maintain supplies ledger cards for each item.  Likewise, require the Municipal General Services Officer to maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately make the necessary adjustments in the books in CY 2021 and submit to the Audit Team a certified copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking up said adjustment.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account upon purchase, and to recognize its related expenses only when supported with the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued from the end-users and Supply Officer with attached pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips.

We also recommend that the Supply Officer prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, within five days after the end of each month, supported with pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slips issued to end users as basis in recording the reduction of the Inventory account and its related expense account.

We further recommend that the MDRRMO, MSWDO and other officials, who accepted the relief goods and took charge with its distribution, submit a sworn certification that all the relief goods were fully distributed to the intended recipients and that no remaining stocks were in their custody as of December 31, 2020.  Any remaining stocks should be reported to the Accountant for recording in the Inventory account.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the Municipal Planning Development Council update its Annual Investment Plan and indicate therein specific development projects which are viable and necessary, with complete description as to the kind of project, its location and estimated appropriation in order to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor issues a directive to the MPDO, the BAC and the Municipal Engineer, to implement and complete all PPAs during the year that it was identified in the AIP.

We recommend that the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant seek the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan, by way of supplemental legislative authorization, so as to legitimize the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We also recommend that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, thru the MDRRM Officer, include always in the LDRRMFIP, under a separate caption, the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF to ensure that disbursements therefrom are covered with appropriate authority.

We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer take the initiative to open and maintain a separate bank account as Special Trust Fund for the unexpended LDRRMF in prior years and transfer the equivalent amount from the General Fund to the new LDRRMF bank account.

Lastly, we recommend that the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant stop using the LDRRM Fund for administrative expenses and provide the MDRRM Office with the funds for such expenses in its annual budget to avoid audit disallowances.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit within three (3) days, the following:

a written explanation on why the requirements mandated in GPPB Circular 01-2020 were not complied prior to the award and payment of the transactions listed in Table 1; and
BAC Resolution recommending the award of the contracts, Notices of Award, Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit, and the Tax Returns and the Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) of contracts above P500,000.00 of the transactions listed in Table 1, otherwise, the transactions shall be issued with Notice of Suspension.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer process transactions only when duly supported with the prescribed necessary documents to avoid audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman, through its Secretariat, post in the PhilGEPS and GPPB website the Purchase Order, Contracts and other pertinent documents on the procurement activities of the Municipality of Mayorga.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure the BAC’s compliance with the on-line posting requirements in Republic Act No. 9184 and other relevant issuances.

6.6	We recommended that the all Accountable Officers (Municipal Treasurer, collectors and disbursing officers be required to update cashbooks and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

Henceforth, management strictly adhere to the provisions of the annual General Appropriations Act on the minimum monthly net take-home pay.

Require the concerned officials involved in approving personal loans to control the loan amount that an employee could avail of by considering the net monthly net take-home pay.

Require the personnel in charge of preparing the payrolls and disbursement vouchers for the payment of claims for Personnel Services to use as guide the provisions of the annual General Appropriations Act in prioritizing the authorized deductions that can be charged against the employees’ claims for Personnel Services, subject to the monthly net take-home pay limitation.

Management, through the Office of the Municipal Accountant, inform in writing the concerned employees on authorized and other deductions that can no longer be deducted from their salaries due to monthly net take-home pay limitation.

We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan:

Update immediately the Municipal Tax Ordinance to conform with the demands of time in strict compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 and its IRR.

Henceforth, update the Municipal Tax Ordinance every after five (5) years pursuant to Article 281 of the IRR of RA 7160.

We recommend that:

The Collectors/Tellers turn over their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer in strict adherence to Sections 60(A) and 61(B) Of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

The deputized Collectors/Tellers remit their collection daily to the regular Revenue Collection Clerk who is accountable therefor.

The Municipal Treasurer issue cash tickets to the Revenue Collector only after the full remittance of collections from the previously issued cash tickets.

Management assign additional personnel who are regular employees to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
We recommend that the individual Collectors/Tellers maintain and update the Cashbook – Teller/Collector (LTO Form 9) prescribed in Section 63(F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommend that:

The Municipal Accountant update the subsidiary accounting records and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a certified copy thereof at the end of each month.

The Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances with the subsidiary accounting records every end of the month in strict adherence to the provisions of Section 63 (F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor the Reconciliation Statements monthly.

We recommend that management:

Effect the immediate return of the unutilized DAP fund of P3 million to the Bureau of Treasury in line with the Supreme Court en banc Decision promulgated on July 1, 2014, as modified in its Resolution promulgated on February 3, 2015.

Establish internal control mechanism to effectively and efficiently monitor the receipt and the utilization of government funds.

Encourage close coordination among the different departments.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason:
The incumbent Municipal Mayor reasoned that the municipality is in dire need of the much needed fund to augment its meager resources for various development projects. He informed us that he has been coordinating with concerned government agencies so that the municipality could eventually be authorized to use the DAP fund to finance various development projects.  He committed to return the fund to the Bureau of Treasury should his requests be denied.

Recommendation (a) is reiterated in Part 2 of this report.

Management, through the HRMO, devise a system to protect the Biometric Finger scanners against loss or damages due to improper use.

We recommend that henceforth, the Inventory Committee undertake an annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October 31 of each ensuing year.

We recommend that:

Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, General Services Officer, and LDRRMC insure yearly with the GSIS the LGU’s insurable properties based on the annual inventory of insurable properties, using the unexpended/unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE funds.
 
Management appropriate in its budget for the payment of premiums for adequate insurance coverage with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS of the LGU’s insurable assets.

We recommend that:

Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job order personnel through Automated Teller Machine instead of cash.

Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for the installation of ATMs nearby, and for the meantime, request from the bank in order to avail the services of mobile ATM.

We recommend that:

a.      Henceforth, management strictly adhere to the provisions of the annual General Appropriations Act on the minimum monthly net take-home pay.

b.	Require the concerned officials involved in approving personal loans to control the loan amount that an employee could avail of by considering the net monthly net take-home pay.

c.	Require the personnel in charge of preparing the payrolls and disbursement vouchers for the payment of claims for Personnel Services to use as guide the provisions of the annual General Appropriations Act in prioritizing the authorized deductions that can be charged against the employees’ claims for Personnel Services, subject to the monthly net take-home pay limitation.

d.	Management, through the Office of the Municipal Accountant, inform in writing the concerned employees on authorized and other deductions that can no longer be deducted from their salaries due to monthly net take-home pay limitation.

We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan:

a.       Update immediately the Municipal Tax Ordinance to conform with the demands of time in strict compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 and its IRR.

b.         Henceforth, update the Municipal Tax Ordinance every after five (5) years pursuant to Article 281 of the IRR of RA 7160.

We recommend that:

The Collectors/Tellers turn over their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer in strict adherence to Sections 60(A) and 61(B) Of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

The deputized Collectors/Tellers remit their collection daily to the regular Revenue Collection Clerk who is accountable therefor.

The Municipal Treasurer issue cash tickets to the Revenue Collector only after the full remittance of collections from the previously issued cash tickets.

Management assign additional personnel who are regular employees to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
We recommend that the individual Collectors/Tellers maintain and update the Cashbook – Teller/Collector (LTO Form 9) prescribed in Section 63(F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommend that:

The Municipal Accountant update the subsidiary accounting records and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a certified copy thereof at the end of each month.

The Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances with the subsidiary accounting records every end of the month in strict adherence to the provisions of Section 63 (F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor the Reconciliation Statements monthly.

We recommend that:
 
The Municipal Accountant coordinate immediately with the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, on the details of the reported GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2016 and submit to the Auditor the schedule of check disbursement details.

The GAD Focal Person coordinate with the previous GAD Focal Person and submit to the Auditor the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2016 using the prescribed format.

Henceforth, the GAD Focal Person submit the DILG- approved GAD Plan and Budget for the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report monthly using the format prescribed in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and other related rules and regulations.

Henceforth, the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and the Municipal Accountant meet monthly in order to update the GAD Fund utilization and accomplishment and for the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the schedule of the corresponding check disbursement details.

We recommend that management:

a. 	Effect the immediate return of the unutilized DAP fund of P3 million to the Bureau of Treasury in line with the Supreme Court en banc Decision promulgated on July 1, 2014, as modified in its Resolution promulgated on February 3, 2015.

b. 	Establish internal control mechanism to effectively and efficiently monitor the receipt and the utilization of government funds.

c. 	Encourage close coordination among the different departments.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

The incumbent Municipal Mayor reasoned that the municipality is in dire need of the much needed fund to augment its meager resources for various development projects. He informed us that he has been coordinating with concerned government agencies so that the municipality could eventually be authorized to use the DAP fund to finance various development projects.  He committed to return the fund to the Bureau of Treasury should his requests be denied.

Recommendation (a) is reiterated in Part 2 of this report.

observations.managementAction: RAAF for the 4th Quarter was submitted on January 25, 2017
observations.reason:
Lack of personnel in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and initiate the conduct of physical inventory of properties for CY 2012 and every year thereafter, pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual of LGUs, Vol. I
observations.managementAction: Please see CY 2014 Finding No. 14
observations.reason: Please see CY 2014 Finding No. 14

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the General Services or Municipal Treasurer to receive all goods procured and delivered by acknowledging/signing on the delivery receipt and temporary recording of the deliveries upon arrival the goods/articles to the LGU premises which shall be subject to the inspection to determine conformity with the specifications as required under Section 114, COA Circular No. 92-386. 

Likewise, instruct the Municipal General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the COA a duplicate copy of the AIR within 24 hours after inspection and acceptance of goods or advance copy thereof for every delivery made/received in accordance with the guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that AIRs are acknowledged
observations.reason: AIRs not furnished to COA within 24 hours after inspection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert earnest efforts and look into the matter in order to determine the causes of the habitual delay. He should direct the prompt submission of required sports in compliance with their duties and responsibilities and as mandated by the prescribed rules and regulations.

Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant strictly enforce submission of reports within the period prescribed under Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006, Section 8 of COA Circular No. 94-001 and Chapter 4, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Please see CY 2015 Finding No. 11
observations.reason: Please see CY 2015 Finding No. 11

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters and give the accountable officers a time frame for the submission of the required documents to facilitate the processing and recording of the liquidations made.

Henceforth, the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 should be strictly enforced.
observations.managementAction: Some cash advances granted in CY 2016 were reported as liquidated in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Cash advances granted in prior years were not monitored due to unavailability of prior years’ detailed records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain an updated Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for each class of allotment pursuant to Sec 2.2 of COA Circular No. 2006-002 and Section 69, 70 and 73 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly reports within the prescribed period pursuant to Sections 69 and 73 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Accountant, the SAAOB Jan June 2016 was submitted on September 29, 2016 while that for July to Dec 2016 was submitted on January 25, 2017
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should require all department heads to make initial review of claims pertaining to his/her office to check completeness of supporting documents. Direct the Municipal Accountant to review all claims prior to approval by the Mayor and payment by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Assigned a staff to conduct pre-audit
observations.reason: Not all payments to various claims were duly supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant review the records and adjust the books of
accounts.
b. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant review the journal entries
made to recognize the transaction using as guide the Revised Chart of
Accounts for LGUs.

We recommend that:
a. Management submit the written request for write-off of dormant
receivables and unliquidated cash advances duly supported with the
requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
b. The Municipal Accountant adjust the books of accounts to restore the
beginning balances derecognized amounting to P4,853,667.65 and
P2,435,277.68 for the accounts Advances to Officers of and Employees
and Advances to Special Disbursing Officer, respectively.
c. The Municipal Accountant comply with all the requirements of COA
Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting journal entries to
revert the adjustments made.
b. The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the previous Municipal
Accountant-Designate and the Municipal Treasurer in verifying the
records in order to determine the cause of the error as basis in the
preparation of the adjusting entries to correct the books of accounts.

We recommend that:
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer immediately
transfer the reported unexpended/unobligated balance of the
DRRMF-MOOE in CY 2016 amounting to P563,835.00 from the
General Fund to Trust Fund Books.
d. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer, LDRRMO and the Municipal
Accountant coordinate with each other in updating monthly the
LDRRMF utilization in order to determine and transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balances of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from
the General Fund to the Trust Fund Books on or before the last
banking day of the current year.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant verify the records and prepare the
adjusting entries to correct the books of accounts.
b. The Municipal Accountant verify as to the correctness of the reports
submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to include the verification
whether the reported deposits are actual and previously accounted as
collections.
c. Management augment the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with
regular employees.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer review the correctness of his Reports of
Collections and Deposits and cashbook based on the official receipts
and bank-validated deposit slips and ensure that the documents are
reported under the correct fund.
b. The Municipal Accountant verify the records and prepare the
adjusting entries to correct the books of accounts.
c. The Municipal Accountant review the correctness of the Municipal
Treasurer’s Report of Collections and Deposits based on the official
receipts and bank-validated deposit slips submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer as basis in the subsequent recognition in the books of
accounts.
d. Management augment the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with
regular employees.

We recommend that management return immediately to the Bureau of
Treasury the unutilized DAP fund of P3 million in line with the Supreme
Court en banc Decision promulgated on July 1, 2014, as modified in its
Resolution promulgated on February 3, 2015.

This is without prejudice to the taking of appropriate measures due to the
continuous failure to return the DAP fund to the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor on the fifth day of the
following month the Consolidated Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms and the supporting Reports of Accountability for
Accountable Forms.
b. The Municipal Treasurer include all accountable forms including
checks in the RAAF and CRAAF.
c. Management assign additional personnel who are regular employees
to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer submit weekly the financial transaction
documents and reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
b. The Municipal Accountant immediately record the submitted reports
and supporting documents in the books of accounts and submit the
financial transaction documents within the first ten (10) days of the
ensuing month.
c. Management assign additional regular employees to the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that management ensure the optimal use of the 20%
development fund to help achieve the desired socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of the
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and other laws, rules and
regulations on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development
fund.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of
Disallowance, if warranted, upon post-audit of subject transactions.

We recommend that management provide storage space and/or garage for the
protection of the farm tractor against fast deterioration and cause its immediate
repair so it can be utilized for agricultural programs of the municipality.

We recommend that:
a. Management require the SB Secretary to turn-over the laptop with
the installed software for the Biometric Finger Scanner and replace
it with another laptop for his office use.
b. Management require the optimal use of the two units of Biometric
Finger Scanners by all permanent officials and employees, co-
terminus, casual, contractual, and Job Order workers of the agency.
c. Management, through the HRMO, require the submission of the
printout from the biometrics as supporting document of the manual
Daily Time Record to be submitted regularly by all permanent
officials and employees, co-terminus, casual, contractual, and Job
Order workers of the agency.
d. Management, through the HRMO, devise a system to protect the
Biometric Finger scanners against loss or damages due to improper
use.

We recommend that henceforth, the Inventory Committee undertake an
annual inventory of all insurable property of the agency and submit the
report to GSIS and furnish a copy to the COA Auditor on or before October
31 of each ensuing year.

We recommend that:
a. Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, General Services
Officer, and LDRRMC insure yearly with the GSIS the LGU’s insurable
properties based on the annual inventory of insurable properties, using
the unexpended/unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE funds.
b. Management appropriate in its budget for the payment of premiums for
adequate insurance coverage with the General Insurance Fund of the
GSIS of the LGU’s insurable assets.

We recommend that:
a. Management pay the salaries, wages, and other monetary benefits of
the agency’s elective officials, regular, casual, contractual and job
order personnel through Automated Teller Machine instead of cash.
b. Management coordinate with nearby agencies and with the AGDB for
the installation of ATMs nearby, and for the meantime, request from
the bank in order to avail the services of mobile ATM.

We recommend that:
a. Henceforth, management strictly adhere to the provisions of the
annual General Appropriations Act on the minimum monthly net
take-home pay.
b. Require the concerned officials involved in approving personal loans
to control the loan amount that an employee could avail of by
considering the net monthly net take-home pay.
c. Require the personnel in charge of preparing the payrolls and
disbursement vouchers for the payment of claims for Personnel
Services to use as guide the provisions of the annual General
Appropriations Act in prioritizing the authorized deductions that
can be charged against the employees’ claims for Personnel Services,
subject to the monthly net take-home pay limitation.
d. Management, through the Office of the Municipal Accountant,
inform in writing the concerned employees on authorized and other
deductions that can no longer be deducted from their salaries due to
monthly net take-home pay limitation.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the ceiling set in the
DBM National Budget Circulars and other related rules and regulations.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or
Disallowance, when warranted, upon post audit of subject transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the submission
of Official Receipts issued by taxi operators instead of RER for claims for
taxi fares.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of
Disallowance, if warranted, upon post-audit of subject transactions.

We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan:
a. Update immediately the Municipal Tax Ordinance to conform with
the demands of time in strict compliance with the pertinent
provisions of RA No. 7160 and its IRR.
b. Henceforth, update the Municipal Tax Ordinance every after five
(5) years pursuant to Article 281 of the IRR of RA 7160.

This audit observation does not require the issuance of Notice of Suspension
or Notice of Disallowance, as this pertains to deficiency in the revenue
system.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all collections to authorized
depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
b. The Municipal Treasurer present the details of check deposits on the
check details breakdown portion of the check deposit slips instead of
using the LRUs.
c. Management make representation with the Land Bank of the
Philippines to secure a copy of the bank-validated LRUs referred to
in the check deposit slips for CYs 2014 to 2016.
d. Management assign additional personnel to the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that:
a. The Collectors/Tellers turn over their collections daily to the
Liquidating Officer in strict adherence to Sections 60(A) and 61(B)
Of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.
b. The deputized Collectors/Tellers remit their collection daily to the
regular Revenue Collection Clerk who is accountable therefor.
c. The Municipal Treasurer issue cash tickets to the Revenue
Collector only after the full remittance of collections from the
previously issued cash tickets.
d. Management assign additional personnel who are regular
employees to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that the individual Collectors/Tellers maintain and
update the Cashbook – Teller/Collector (LTO Form 9) prescribed in Section
63(F) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommend that:
a. The Municipal Accountant update the subsidiary accounting
records and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a certified copy
thereof at the end of each month.
b. The Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances with the
subsidiary accounting records every end of the month in strict
adherence to the provisions of Section 63 (F) of the Local Treasury
Operations Manual.
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit to
the Auditor the Reconciliation Statements monthly.

We recommend that:
e. The Municipal Accountant coordinate immediately with the GAD
Focal Person, the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer,
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, on the details of
the reported GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2016 and submit
to the Auditor the schedule of check disbursement details.
f. The GAD Focal Person coordinate with the previous GAD Focal
Person and submit to the Auditor the GAD Accomplishment Report
for CY 2016 using the prescribed format.
g. Henceforth, the GAD Focal Person submit the DILG- approved GAD
Plan and Budget for the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report
monthly using the format prescribed in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and other related rules and
regulations.
h. Henceforth, the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator, and the Municipal Accountant meet monthly in order to
update the GAD Fund utilization and accomplishment and for the
Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the schedule of the
corresponding check disbursement details.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of
Disallowance, if warranted, upon post-audit of subject transactions.
